copy.x: 25 years ago today: Apple opened its first two retail stores. Business Week's headline that week was literally 'Sorry Steve: Here's Why Apple Stores Won't Work.' 🍎 The first weekend pulled in $1 million. Every detail of the concept was designed in Cupertino.
copy.threads:
On this day in 2001, Apple opened its first two retail stores — and pretty much every analyst predicted disaster. Business Week ran 'Sorry Steve: Here's Why Apple Stores Won't Work.' 🍎

The open wooden tables, the demo stations, the Genius Bar — all designed back in Cupertino by Ron Johnson and Steve Jobs. By 2012, those stores were generating more revenue per square foot than any retailer in history.

#SiliconValley #Cupertino #TechHistory
